mentions.note: Rallied from 862 to 915; December comps beat expectations with digital acceleration — best beat in years. Estimates now too low; expect earnings bumps and price target increases.

Trading at 50x earnings (vs historical high of 47x); re-up rate weak but numbers coming through. Stock bounced on technicals after pantry-stocking discount already priced in. Stay long despite valuation concerns.
Value-oriented warehouse club; tangible products; understable business; high demand.
Special place in Cramer's heart but card renewal rate declining—younger e-commerce users falling out of love; key metric to watch.
Caller Jonathan: solid earnings, can head to new highs; hold. When gasoline spikes, historically best time to buy Costco. Wait for pullback after recent spike.
Buy in this difficult economic environment; represents recession-play/consumer-staples strength. Recovered from January lows, new CEO in place. One to own, not trade—bullish for extended period.
Down on Day 2; expected strength in retail leadership not materializing.
Two catalysts: cheapest gas drives card sign-ups (money made there) and inflation-fighter positioning; likes despite recent trimming.
Nico's portfolio: cheap gas not great for them but high gas drives membership card sign-ups—how they make money; great retail holding.
Passing tariff refunds back to members; customer-first ethos; position held in Cramer trust; refusing to play political games.
Trades 49x earnings (never cheap, always terrific). One of longest holdings for Charitable Trust. Would not buy right here; it's sensational but already priced.
Gotten red hot; strong performer. Prefer for long run over BJ's Wholesale Club; retailer divergence evident.
Buy some here with hope for pullback to 45x earnings. Defensive quality but not urgent entry; accumulate on dips.
